content.title: Asian Stocks Shrug Off Tariff Jitters
content.summary: Asian stocks rose broadly, with South Korea and Taiwan setting records, despite renewed trade uncertainty.  Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index rose 2.5%.   South Korea’s Kospi composite closed at a record high, as did its biggest component, Samsung Electronics.

content.title: Indian Stocks Advance After U.S. Agrees to Slash Tariffs
content.summary: Indian stocks jumped after President Trump agreed to slash tariffs on the country. The U.S. is lowering levies on Indian goods to 18% after the two nations struck a trade pact and Trump said New Delhi, led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, agreed to stop buying Russian oil.

content.title: EU-India Trade Deal by the Numbers
content.summary: That's the annual saving equivalent the European Union expects as a result of its newly struck trade deal with India.  The EU says the pact is set to eliminate or reduce tariffs for more than 90% of its goods.  It is the latest example of U.S. trading partners seeking to curb their reliance on America by expanding ties with other markets.

Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
content.description:
Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.

Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.

Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

content.title: Indian IT firms face subdued fourth quarter as war, AI concerns persist; weak rupee helps earnings
content.summary: Top Indian information technology firms are set to report another lacklustre quarter, with revenue and profit seen rising around 10% year-on-year largely on ‌a weaker rupee rather than underlying growth, seven brokerages said. Uncertainties due to wars, weak discretionary spending and ‌concerns around artificial intelligence will keep weighing on client budgets, making the revenue forecast for the next fiscal year a key focus for ​investors, they added. Tata Consultancy Services, Infosys, HCLTech and other software services exporters are due to report fourth quarter results starting April 9.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: A person visits WAIC in Shanghai

content.title: Bernstein: Human oversight remains critical despite rapid AI coding integration
content.summary: Investing.com -- Generative AI is not yet driving systematic job losses in the technology sector, according to a report from Bernstein, despite the rapid integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into professional coding workflows. Following 50 hours of intensive interviews with tech professionals globally, analysts found that AI is reshaping role boundaries, but human capital remains "surprisingly resilient," debunking popular narratives of an immediate white-collar "apocalypse."

content.title: PayPal Joins Forces With TCS Blockchain for Faster Invoice Settlements
content.summary: PYPL teams up with TCS Blockchain to speed freight invoice settlements and slash costs for carriers.

content.title: India's TCS urging staff to use AI despite risk to revenue, CEO says
content.summary: Tata Consultancy Services is urging employees to use artificial intelligence tools ‌to deliver work faster and cheaper, even if it ‌eats into the company's revenue, the CEO of India's largest software-services provider ​said on Wednesday.  "We are telling ‌associates that if you ⁠find that you can do something faster, better, cheaper with AI, you should probably go ⁠and tell your customers, even if it cannibalises revenue," CEO K Krithivasan said at the Nasscom Technology and Leadership Forum in ​Mumbai.  TCS' stance mirrors that of smaller rival Wipro, which expects rapid AI adoption to ‌boost rather than shrink demand for ​software service providers.

thumbnail.caption: Illustration shows TCS: Tata Consultancy Services logo

content.title: Disney Planning Layoffs Under New CEO Josh D’Amaro
content.summary: Disney  is preparing to make sizable layoffs in one of the first significant moves under the new chief executive officer,  Josh D’Amaro.  The entertainment company is planning to eliminate as many as 1,000 positions in the coming weeks, according to people familiar with the matter.  Like many Hollywood studios, Disney has been trying to adjust to the smaller profits it earns from streaming compared with what it used to make in linear television, as well as diminished box office and intense competition from such tech companies as  Amazon.com  and Google’s YouTube.

content.title: Bitcoin Climbs to Three-Week High on US-Iran Ceasefire Plan
content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Bitcoin touched a three-week high as global markets turned more optimistic after a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran.The largest cryptocurrency climbed as much as 5% to $72,841, its highest level since March 18, in New York trading, before paring some of the gain. Smaller tokens also notched big gains, with Ether rising as much as 7.5% to $2,273.Stocks surged and the price of the US benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude oil fell below $95, nearing its biggest price drop in
